title: React Uncontrolled Components
tldr: Uncontrolled components store their own internal state in the DOM, not in React state.
Access the current value via a ref (useRef) instead of an onChange handler.
Useful for simple, read-once inputs like file uploads or integrating with non-React code.
React provides defaultValue to set an initial value without controlling the input.
laymanDefinition: An uncontrolled component is like a vending machine. The machine (input) manages its own state internally. You don't need to tell it what to display - you just ask it later what was selected (via ref). Controlled is like a soda fountain where an attendant (React) pushes the button for you.

deepDive.heading: What Is an Uncontrolled Component?
deepDive.text: A form element that manages its own value internally (in the DOM). React does not set the value via state. Instead, you use a ref to read the current value when needed (e.g., on form submission).

deepDive.heading: Default Values with defaultValue
deepDive.text: React provides defaultValue (and defaultChecked for checkboxes) to set an initial value for uncontrolled inputs. This is the only React-controlled aspect - after initial render, the input manages itself.

deepDive.heading: When to Use Uncontrolled
deepDive.text: File inputs (which are inherently uncontrolled), simple forms where you only need values on submit, integrating with non-React libraries that manipulate the DOM directly, and performance-critical scenarios with many inputs.

deepDive.heading: Refs to Read Values
deepDive.text: useRef creates a mutable object with a .current property. Attach to the input via ref prop. Read the value on submit: inputRef.current.value. Refs don't cause re-renders when updated.

interviewAnswer: Uncontrolled components keep their state in the DOM rather than React state. Use useRef to access the input's current value when needed. React sets an initial value via defaultValue, then the browser manages the input. Uncontrolled is simpler for basic forms but lacks the real-time validation and control of controlled components.

interviewQuestions.question: What is an uncontrolled component?
interviewQuestions.answer: A form input that manages its own state in the DOM. React reads the value via ref when needed.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you set an initial value?
interviewQuestions.answer: Use defaultValue prop (not value). React sets initial value, then the DOM takes over.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you read the current value?
interviewQuestions.answer: Via a ref: const inputRef = useRef(); <input ref={inputRef} />. Read: inputRef.current.value.

interviewQuestions.question: When to use uncontrolled over controlled?
interviewQuestions.answer: File inputs, simple submit-only forms, third-party DOM integration, performance optimization for many inputs.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a ref?
interviewQuestions.answer: A mutable object (useRef) that persists across renders. Updating .current doesn't trigger re-render.

interviewQuestions.question: Can uncontrolled inputs have validation?
interviewQuestions.answer: Yes, but validation happens on submit (reading ref value) rather than on each keystroke.

interviewQuestions.question: Can you mix controlled and uncontrolled?
interviewQuestions.answer: React warns if an input switches patterns. Stick to one pattern per input.

interviewQuestions.question: What about initial values for uncontrolled inputs?
interviewQuestions.answer: Only defaultValue works. The value prop is ignored because React doesn't control it.

diagramSvg: (read on submit)Form Submit HandlerAccess ref.current.value

codeExamples.title: Uncontrolled Input with Ref
codeExamples.description: Inputs manage their own state. Refs read values on submit. No onChange or state needed.

codeExamples.title: File Input (Inherently Uncontrolled)
codeExamples.description: File inputs are inherently uncontrolled (read-only value). Use ref to access the selected File object.

mcqQuestions.question: How to read current value?
mcqQuestions.explanation: Use ref to read value when needed.

mcqQuestions.question: When to use uncontrolled?
mcqQuestions.explanation: File inputs and simple submit-only forms.

mcqQuestions.question: What is defaultValue for?
options: Setting initial value for uncontrolled inputs
mcqQuestions.explanation: Sets initial value for uncontrolled inputs.

mcqQuestions.question: Can uncontrolled inputs have real-time validation?
mcqQuestions.explanation: Validation happens on submit when reading ref.
